summary: A monster multi‑track capture of the legendary 2/28/69 Alligator marathon, featuring stellar School Girl and Alligator suite performances despite occasional pitch wobble.
review: The show is widely praised as a peak early‑‘69 performance – the 30‑minute Alligator sequence, a soaring Good Morning School Girl, and a powerhouse The Other One are repeatedly singled out.  The Healy/Betty multi‑track soundboard source delivers clear, instrument‑rich imaging, though several listeners note patchy sections and minor speed/pitch irregularities that have been corrected in newer mixes.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star ratings and highlight exceptional musical moments; a few note recording quirks, pulling the technical score down slightly but keeping the overall rating strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multi‑track soundboard mix is generally clear and well‑balanced with excellent instrument separation. Reviewers mention occasional patchy sections, speed/pitch wobble on The Other One and Drums that required minor correction in newer releases.

standout_songs: Alligator > Drums > Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) > Feedback > And We Bid You Good Night
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon: a pig‑focused first set, an extended second‑set jam suite (Cryptical → The Other One → Dark Star → etc.), and a monumental third set built around the Alligator suite. Reviewers describe the pacing as “monster” throughout, with seamless transitions that keep momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is noted for flowing ideas and being prominent in the mix; several reviewers call his playing ‘inspired’ on this night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh receives praise for owning The Eleven with a powerful bass line.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment within the Alligator suite is highlighted, though some note slight speed/pitch irregularities during the Drums portion.
